data.description:
## Context / Problem

The two weekly, canonical-repo-only GitHub Actions jobs that make **real, billable LLM
calls** surface **no retrievable record of token usage**:

- `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml` → `external` job (weekly `cron "0 6 * * 1"`),
  runs `pytest -m external tests/external` (`external-integration.yml:30-136`).
- `.github/workflows/prompt-eval.yml` → `eval-live` job (weekly `cron "0 7 * * 1"`),
  runs `ev.run_eval(pid, max_cases=cap)` over the eval specs (`prompt-eval.yml:79-100`).

Per-call usage is *already captured in code* — `PydanticAIRunner.run` computes
`usage = _extract_usage(...)` (input/output/cache tokens + `requests`) at
`src/rebar/llm/runner.py:392,394`, logs one INFO line at `runner.py:452-470`, and attaches
it as a private `result["_usage"] = usage` at `runner.py:486`. But that record never reaches
either weekly job: the eval path (`ev.run_eval`, `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py`) returns only
`passed`/`coverage`/`junit` and drops `_usage`; the external `pytest` tier surfaces nothing;
and the INFO log lines are swallowed because `src/rebar/__init__.py` attaches a `NullHandler`
to the `rebar` logger and neither job calls `logging.basicConfig`. The only usage-bearing
path, the `langfuse-trace` job, exports spans to an **ephemeral** Langfuse torn down with
`docker compose ... down -v` each run, so nothing is retrievable afterward.

There is **no token→USD price table anywhere** in the codebase, so this ticket records **token
usage** (input/output/cache tokens + request count), not a dollar figure.

Edit-set (all paths confirmed present unless marked *new*):
- *new* `src/rebar/llm/usage_log.py`
- `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` (720 LOC today; cap is 800 per `.github/module-size-limit.txt`)
- *new* `tests/unit/test_usage_log.py`
- `.github/workflows/prompt-eval.yml`
- `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml`

## Approach

Add one process-level usage sink at the **already-existing** `_usage` seam that is shared by
both jobs (both go through `PydanticAIRunner.run`), so a single hook covers both without any
per-test or per-eval plumbing.

1. New module `src/rebar/llm/usage_log.py`:
   - `record(usage: dict, *, op: str) -> None` — when env var `REBAR_USAGE_LOG` is set to a
     path, append one JSON object per call (`op`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`,
     `cache_read_tokens`, `cache_write_tokens`, `requests`) as a line to that file (JSONL).
     No-op when the env var is unset. Best-effort: it wraps its file write in a `try/except`
     and never raises into the LLM call path (a telemetry sink must not break a run).
   - `summarize(path: str) -> str` — read the JSONL and return a Markdown summary: a totals
     row (summed tokens + total calls) plus a per-`op` breakdown table. A missing or empty
     file returns the single line `No LLM calls recorded.` (so a run with zero LLM calls still
     produces valid, honest summary output).
   - `__main__` CLI: `python -m rebar.llm.usage_log summarize <path>` prints `summarize(path)`
     to stdout, for `>> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"` in CI.
2. `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` — immediately after `result["_usage"] = usage` (`runner.py:486`),
   call `usage_log.record(usage, op=_call_label)` (module-level import). One statement; keeps
   `runner.py` well under the 800-LOC cap.
3. Both workflows (`prompt-eval.yml` `eval-live` job and `external-integration.yml` `external`
   job): set job env `REBAR_USAGE_LOG: ${{ runner.temp }}/usage.jsonl`; after the test/eval
   step (with `if: always()` so a test failure still reports spend), run
   `python -m rebar.llm.usage_log summarize "$REBAR_USAGE_LOG" >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"` and
   upload the raw JSONL via `actions/upload-artifact`. This gives both a human-glanceable job
   summary and a durable, machine-readable artifact.

Rejected alternatives: (a) persisting the `langfuse-trace` spans — requires standing up and
retaining a Langfuse backend + secrets, far heavier than the ask and off the eval job's path
entirely; (b) parsing the runner's INFO log lines out of pytest stdout — fragile and
suppressed by the `NullHandler` today. The `_usage` seam is the one chokepoint already common
to both jobs.

## Scope

- Recording is **token usage only** (input/output/cache tokens + request count). No dollar
  cost is computed — no price table exists in the repo, and adding one is out of scope.
- The sink is **opt-in via `REBAR_USAGE_LOG`**; default (env unset) behavior of the runner and
  of every existing caller is unchanged, so `make test` and normal library use are unaffected.
- Only the two weekly jobs set `REBAR_USAGE_LOG`; the PR/push and Verified lanes are untouched.
- No change to what tests/evals run, to gating, to `REBAR_EVAL_MAX_CASES`, or to any provider
  credential handling.

## Testing

Offline unit tests only (no live LLM; consistent with the `not integration and not external`
default suite). New `tests/unit/test_usage_log.py`:
- `record` appends one well-formed JSON line per call when `REBAR_USAGE_LOG` points at a
  tmp_path file, and is a **no-op** when the env var is unset (assert no file created).
- `summarize` aggregates multiple recorded lines into correct totals and a per-`op` breakdown;
  a missing/empty file yields exactly `No LLM calls recorded.`
- Record-at-the-seam: drive `PydanticAIRunner.run` through the existing offline
  `FunctionModel` model-override path (with `pydantic_ai.models.ALLOW_MODEL_REQUESTS = False`,
  as in `tests/unit/test_llm_temperature.py`) with `REBAR_USAGE_LOG` set to a tmp file, and
  assert a JSONL line is appended — proving the runner call site fires without a live call.
- CLI: invoking `summarize` via the module entry point returns/prints the Markdown summary.

Workflow YAML edits are validated by `python -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open(f))"`
for each changed file (structural parse) in addition to the CI lint that already runs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `src/rebar/llm/usage_log.py` exists exposing `record(usage, *, op)` and
      `summarize(path)` with the semantics above; verified by `pytest tests/unit/test_usage_log.py` passing.
- [ ] With `REBAR_USAGE_LOG` unset, `record(...)` writes no file and the runner path is
      unchanged; verified by the no-op unit test.
- [ ] `PydanticAIRunner.run` appends a usage record when `REBAR_USAGE_LOG` is set; verified by
      the FunctionModel-override unit test asserting a JSONL line was written.
- [ ] `python -m rebar.llm.usage_log summarize <path>` prints the Markdown totals + per-op
      table (or `No LLM calls recorded.` for an empty/missing file); verified by the CLI test.
- [ ] The `eval-live` job (`prompt-eval.yml`) and the `external` job
      (`external-integration.yml`) each set `REBAR_USAGE_LOG`, append `summarize` output to
      `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` (`if: always()`), and upload the JSONL as an artifact; verified by
      `yaml.safe_load` of each file and grep for the three additions.
- [ ] `src/rebar/llm/runner.py` remains ≤ 800 LOC; verified by `wc -l`.
- [ ] `make test`, `make lint`, and `make typecheck` all pass locally; the change lands via
      Gerrit with `Verified +1` and `LLM-Review +1`.
data.title: Record token usage from the two weekly live-LLM jobs (external + eval-live)
